title: rootless container systemd errors when running in container with all capabilities added
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running a rootless container with `cap-add=all`, running systemd inside the container fails.

Failing units:

- sys-kernel-config.mount
- sys-kernel-debug.mount
- systemd-journald-audit.socket

When adding `--privileged`, The following units fail as well:

- proc-sys-fs-binfmt_misc.automount

Furthermore, shutting down the container also gives errors:

```
Failed to set wall message, ignoring: Could not activate remote peer.
Failed to power off system via logind: Could not activate remote peer.
[root@dce9d262111f /]# Error: non zero exit code: 129: OCI runtime error
```

Steps to reproduce the issue:

```
podman run -dt --cap-add=all --privileged fedora:rawhide /lib/systemd/systemd
podman exec -it -l /bin/bash
systemctl status
```

Similar problems can be seen with a ubuntu 20.04 container:

Dockerfile:

```
FROM ubuntu:20.04

RUN apt-get update && \
    DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        systemd \
        systemd-container
```

Failing units (`--privileged` and `cap-add=all`):

- proc-sys-fs-binfmt_misc.automount
- sys-kernel-debug.mount 

Shutting down the container also gives an error:

```
root@9e756e489805:/# systemctl poweroff
root@9e756e489805:/# Error: non zero exit code: 129: OCI runtime error
```

**Output of `podman version`**:

comments.body:
I take it you are running rootless container.  Systemd might be attempting to do things that are just impossible for a rootless container.  IE Rootless containers are only allowed to mount procfs, Fuse, sysfs, tmpfs file systems.  Anything else requires real CAP_SYS_ADMIN.

This is probably what is blocking the mount unit files.

Looking at the debug and config mount units, there are two conditions for them to be started:

sys-kernel-debug.mount:

```
ConditionPathExists=/sys/kernel/debug
ConditionCapability=CAP_SYS_RAWIO
```

sys-kernel-config.mount:

```
ConditionPathExists=/sys/kernel/config
ConditionCapability=CAP_SYS_RAWIO
```

Maybe we can hide these in rootless containers so that systemd won't try to mount them?

Another idea I had was to have some way to have `cap-add=all` only give capabilities to rootless containers that it actually can do something with but that might be impossible if adding some capabilities does allow a container to do more than it normally would but not everything (e.g. you might be able to do extra stuff with `CAP_SYS_RAWIO`, just not mount stuff from `/sys/kernel`).

The other two units (automount and audit.socket) fail with a generic (Result: resources) error, I'll investigate why those fail exactly once I have some more free time.

My ultimate goal here is to hopefully have systemd in podman containers just work (without errors) regardless of the way podman is configured (root/rootless, capabilities, privileged, ...)
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

The problem is podman has no idea which Capabilties allow what, and this changes all the time.
I don't see us adding these features, and believe that the user should be required to figure this out.
